confirmed.title: AiImageInput dropzone prompt label hardcoded English (not localized)
confirmed.detail: The file-dropzone <span> renders a raw English template literal `Click or drop image (${accept !== '' ? accept : 'any'})` with no t() wrapper, even though the translator `t = useArqelTranslations()` is in scope and used everywhere else in this same component (e.g. line 178 'Analyze with AI', line 204 'File too large', line 267 analyze_error_http). This is the primary visible call-to-action of the image field before a file is chosen, so non-EN admins see English. The accept-hint fallback word 'any' (when accept is empty) is also English. The ledger covers this file's 'File too large' validation, file-size MB/KB/B units, and preview img alt — but NOT this dropzone prompt. Suggest: t('arqel.ai.image_dropzone', 'Click or drop image (:accept)', { accept: accept !== '' ? accept : t('arqel.ai.image_accept_any', 'any') }).
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at packages-js/ai/src/AiImageInput.tsx:338. The file-dropzone <span> renders `file !== null ? file.name : \`Click or drop image (${accept !== '' ? accept : 'any'})\`` — a raw English template literal with no t() wrapper. The translator `t = useArqelTranslations()` is in scope and used immediately adjacent (line 346 aria-label 'Image file', line 355 'Selected preview' alt), so this is a genuine missed localization, not an out-of-scope context. It is the primary visible call-to-action of the image field before a file is selected, so non-EN admins see English; the 'any' accept-hint fallback is also hardcoded English. Not vendored (packages-js/ai, not ui/src/shadcn). Ledger has 5 AiImageInput entries — line 193 'File too large' (R5), file-size MB/KB/B units (R5), line 230 'Missing analyze URL' (R7), line 350 preview img alt (R7) — but NONE covers the line-338 dropzone prompt. Genuinely novel, user-facing, reproduces.

confirmed.title: RepeaterField itemLabel template serialized verbatim — never passed through trans()/localizeLabel()
confirmed.suggested_key: (app-must-key: localize the static portion of the itemLabel template via trans() before the {{label}} substitution)
confirmed.detail: RepeaterField::getTypeSpecificProps() serializes the user-supplied itemLabel template ('itemLabel' => $this->itemLabel) verbatim. itemLabel is a developer-set, user-facing per-row heading template (e.g. 'Address {{label}}') rendered in the admin panel above each repeater item. Unlike Field::$label (ledger-localized via localizeLabel) and Field::$placeholder/$helperText (ledger entry packages/fields/src/Field.php:206, flagged & fixed), this label is shipped to the frontend with no localization hook, so a translation-key itemLabel cannot resolve and an English literal cannot be translated. App-must-key consistency gap analogous to the placeholder/helperText fix. Weaker than the StateFilter finding because the value carries {{label}} interpolation, making a naive trans() of the whole string awkward — a localize-then-interpolate pattern (or localizing only the static prefix) is the appropriate fix.
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/fields-advanced/src/Types/RepeaterField.php:187. getTypeSpecificProps() serializes the developer-set, user-facing itemLabel template ('itemLabel' => $this->itemLabel) verbatim, with no localizeLabel()/trans() pass-through. itemLabel (set via itemLabel() at line 139) is the collapsed-item header template rendered in the admin panel (e.g. "Address {{label}}"). The base Field class applies localizeLabel() to label (line 185), placeholder (getPlaceholder, line 210) and helperText (getHelperText, line 217) so the active-request locale applies at serialization time — but RepeaterField's subclass-specific itemLabel prop bypasses that entirely. A translation-key itemLabel cannot resolve and an English literal cannot be translated. The {{...}} interpolation tokens do not block a fix: localizeLabel() uses trans() which returns the key unchanged when no translation exists, and the {{label}} tokens are interpolated client-side AFTER serialization, so a localize-then-interpolate pattern works cleanly.

Not a duplicate: grep of the R1-R38 ledger for "itemLabel" and "RepeaterField" returned zero hits. The analogous ledger entry (packages/fields/src/Field.php:206, round 8) covers only base-Field placeholder/helperText; itemLabel has its own independent serialization path in getTypeSpecificProps() and is not reached by that base fix. Not vendored (fields-advanced, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*). First-party user-facing PHP label = in scope.

Confidence med, not high: the value is an interpolation template, making it a weaker/more nuanced case than a plain label (the finding acknowledges this), and a localize-then-interpolate fix is slightly more involved than a straight localizeLabel() wrap. But the framework convention of localizing user-facing labels at serialization time is genuinely and inconsistently skipped here.
